evidence.page_title: Home and Away's Samara Weaving films bloody scenes for new ...
evidence.title: Samara Weaving sports bloodied face in her new movie Mayhem
evidence.description: Samara Weaving sported a bloodied face and neck as she filmed scenes for her new movie, Mayhem, on Tuesday.
evidence.text:
Samara Weaving sports a blood splattered face and neck as she films scenes for new movie Mayhem with The Walking Dead star Steven Yeun
She was the fresh-faced bikini babe on Home And Away, but Samara Weaving was sporting a very different look on Tuesday evening.
Appearing battered and bloodied, the 24-year-old Australian actress shared a snap to Instagram of herself posing in a plunging red gown alongside her co-star Steven Yeun on set of their new movie, Mayhem.
Originally shared by their film-maker Joe Lynch, the two stars appear to be strategically looking around a bloodied corner as they filmed scenes for the action thriller.
Scroll down for video
New flick! Samara Weaving and her co-star Steven Yeun sport bloodied faces as they film scenes for their new movie, Mayhem
In the caption, Joe wrote: 'DAY 16: You never know what's around the corner here...WEEK 3 is WRAPPED B*****s!!!'
The movie follows the story of a virus transforming its victims to act out their wildest impulses.
It’s not understood what Samara's character, Melanie Cross, is penned to do in the upcoming horror flick.
Snapping away: The co-stars looked downcast as they held hands on set of their new indie thriller
The movie will be a major move for the blonde actress, who since breaking out on Ash vs Evil has largely stuck to making a few handful appearances on the small screen, as well as starring in a string of movies including Bad Girl, Before Dawn and Monster Trucks.
Her co-star, Steven, will also step up from his TV roles in The Walking Dead and Voltron: Legendary Defender.
Speaking about the movie, Joe told The Hollywood Reporter: ‘It’s not every day you get to throw action, sci-fi, horror, drama and even satire in the genre blender and hit puree, but Matias’ script, combined with a gung-ho cast and crew — especially Steven and Samara — will make for a deliciously dangerous and exciting cinematic smoothie ... with a hell of a kick.'
Form Aussie soap to the big screen: Samara, who is the niece of Hugo Weaving, first found fame on long-running Channel Seven soap Home And Away playing the character of Indigo Walker from 2009 to 2013
Samara, who is the niece of Hugo Weaving, first found fame on long-running Channel Seven soap Home And Away playing the character of Indigo Walker from 2009 to 2013.
She recently wrapped filming on her first Hollywood movie, The Babysitter, described by IMDB as a 'coming-of-age horror story'.
The horror theme continues for the actress, who has just been announced to play the lead role of Grace Becket in the zombie film Before Dawn.
Samara will also appear in the 2017 sci-fi film, Monster Trucks, with Ice Age director Chris Wedge.

evidence.page_title: Ash vs Evil Dead actor Samara Weaving caught up in Donald Trump ...
evidence.title: Ash vs Evil Dead actor Samara Weaving caught up in Donald Trump internet hoax
evidence.description: The 'bloodied' face of former Home and Away actress Samara Weaving has been used by supporters of Donald Trump to discredit their political opponents.
evidence.text:
This was published 9 years ago
Ash vs Evil Dead actor Samara Weaving caught up in Donald Trump internet hoax
Updated ,first published
Former Home and Away actress Samara Weaving has been embroiled in a social media hoax involving United States presidential candidate Donald Trump.
The bloodied face of the Australian on set of an American horror-comedy series has been used as evidence by Trump supporters of the ugly face of the US presidential election campaign.
Conservative Nation, a pro-Trump supporters' network, tweeted the image on June 6 beneath the headline: "Here's what happened to female Trump supporter when she met 'peaceful' and 'tolerant' liberals."
It became conflated with a scuffle at a Trump campaign event in San Jose in California the day before.
The image in fact was taken during production of Ash vs Evil Dead, a US television series developed by Sam and Ivan Raimi, which premiered last October.
Weaving plays Heather, the sole survivor of a hiking group, who is killed off in an Evil Dead plague.
Hair and make-up artist Hannah Wilson originally showcased the image on Instagram in January depicting realistic special effects, including a bloodied eye and claw marks. The account has since been made private. But Weaving's Instagram also shows the make-up artistry.
The misuse of the image was quickly questioned, exposed as false, and reported by Snopes, The Daily Dot, zines dedicated to reporting the internet.
Weaving is the niece of Hugo Weaving and broke into mainstream acting playing Indi Walker in the soap opera Home and Away. It isn't known whether Weaving is even a Trump supporter.
image_captions: Australian actress Samara Weaving has unwittingly become embroiled in a pro-Donald Trump campaign.Caroline McCredie/Getty Images
Australian actress Samara Weaving has unwittingly become embroiled in a pro-Donald Trump campaign.

evidence.page_title: Samara Weaving sports bloodied face on the set of Ash vs Evil Dead
evidence.title: Samara Weaving sports bloodied makeup on the set for Ash vs Evil Dead
evidence.description: Appearing battered and bloodied, the 23-year-old shared a snap from the makeup chair on the set of Ash vs Evil Dead, a comedy horror show now in its second season.
evidence.text:
Now that's scary! Samara Weaving sports bloodied face and a bruised eye while on the set of Ash vs Evil Dead
She was a fresh-faced beauty on Home and Away, but Samara Weaving was sporting a slightly different look on Tuesday.
Appearing battered and bloodied, the 23-year-old shared a snap from the makeup chair on the set of Ash vs Evil Dead, a comedy horror show now in its second season.
The Australian actress played the role of Heather in three episodes in the show's first season, with fans expressing how happy they were she was returning.
Scroll down for video
It's only makeup! Samara Weaving sported bloodied face and a bruised eye while on the set of Ash vs Evil Dead on Tuesday (picture shared by the makeup artist Hannah Wilson)
Originally shared by the makeup artist, Hannah Wilson, the snap depicts exceptionally realistic effects, including a bloodied eye and what appear to be claw marks on Samara's decolletage.
In the caption, Hannah wrote: 'No matter what we did to her, she was still a stone cold fox'.
Samara, who is the niece of Hollywood actor Hugo Weaving, first found fame on Home and Away as the character of Indigo Walker from 2009 to 2013.
Fresh-faced: Samara won hearts as the natural beauty Indigo Walker on Home and Away from 2009 to 2013
Big break: The 23-year-old has just wrapped filming on her first Hollywood movie, The Babysitter
She's recently wrapped filming on her first Hollywood movie, The Babysitter, described by IMDB as a 'coming-of-age horror story'.
Working with director Joseph McGinty Nichol, known in the business as simply McG, should do wonders for her career, which was off to a slow start.
The Charlies Angels filmmaker is known for launching the careers of Rachel Bilson, Mischa Barton, Adam Brody and Benjamin McKenzie on The OC.
The theme continues: Samara has just been announced as the lead in another horror film, zombie flick Before Dawn
He has also worked on hit TV shows such as Supernatural, Chuck and producing the video clip to The Offspring's iconic 90s hit Pretty Fly For A White Guy.
The horror theme continues for the actress, who has just been announced to play the lead role of Grace Becket in the zombie film Before Dawn.
Samara will also appear in the 2017 sci-fi film, Monster Trucks, with Ice Age director Chris Wedge.
Famous family: Samara is the niece of Hollywood actor Hugo Weaving

evidence.title: Ash vs Evil Dead actor Samara Weaving caught up in Donald Trump internet hoax
evidence.description: The 'bloodied' face of former Home and Away actress Samara Weaving has been used by supporters of Donald Trump to discredit their political opponents.
evidence.text:
This was published 9 years ago
Ash vs Evil Dead actor Samara Weaving caught up in Donald Trump internet hoax
Updated ,first published
Former Home and Away actress Samara Weaving has been embroiled in a social media hoax involving United States presidential candidate Donald Trump.
The bloodied face of the Australian on set of an American horror-comedy series has been used as evidence by Trump supporters of the ugly face of the US presidential election campaign.
Conservative Nation, a pro-Trump supporters' network, tweeted the image on June 6 beneath the headline: "Here's what happened to female Trump supporter when she met 'peaceful' and 'tolerant' liberals."
It became conflated with a scuffle at a Trump campaign event in San Jose in California the day before.
The image in fact was taken during production of Ash vs Evil Dead, a US television series developed by Sam and Ivan Raimi, which premiered last October.
Weaving plays Heather, the sole survivor of a hiking group, who is killed off in an Evil Dead plague.
Hair and make-up artist Hannah Wilson originally showcased the image on Instagram in January depicting realistic special effects, including a bloodied eye and claw marks. The account has since been made private. But Weaving's Instagram also shows the make-up artistry.
The misuse of the image was quickly questioned, exposed as false, and reported by Snopes, The Daily Dot, zines dedicated to reporting the internet.
Weaving is the niece of Hugo Weaving and broke into mainstream acting playing Indi Walker in the soap opera Home and Away. It isn't known whether Weaving is even a Trump supporter.

evidence.page_title: Bloodied 'Trump supporter' in hoax photo is 'Ash vs Evil Dead ...
evidence.title: Bloodied ‘Trump supporter’ in hoax photo is ‘Ash vs Evil Dead’ actress Samara Weaving - National | Globalnews.ca
evidence.description: The bloodied face of a young, blonde-haired woman has been held up as an example of the violence endured by Donald Trump supporters at the hands of Liberals.
evidence.text:
The bloodied face of a young, blonde-haired woman has been held up as an example of the violence endured by Donald Trump supporters at the hands of “Liberals”.
But the image shared more than 30,000 times on Facebook is not that of an innocent Trump supporter, battered by vicious Democrats, rather a woman fending off the Evil Dead.
Australian actress Samara Weaving is the blood-covered woman and the photo is actually from the makeup chair on set of the U.S. television series Ash vs Evil Dead.
The actress posted it on her Instagram account in early January, while working on the horror/comedy series — a spin-off of the 1981 Sam Raimi-directed cult classic film, The Evil Dead.
https://www.instagram.com/p/BAI1LiFCiST/
But a Facebook user identified as Greg McCurdy shared the months-old image on his account on June 4, claiming that Weaving’s appearance was “The result of Fascism in America… simply because she was a Trump supporter.”
There were indeed violent clashes involving anti-Trump protesters last week outside a rally in San Jose, California, where punches —and eggs — were thrown at the presumptive Republican presidential nominee’s supporters.
But Weaving has no horse in this election race, pointing out on Twitter she’s not even a U.S. citizen and can’t vote in this fall’s election.
Get daily National news
That post caught the eye of the star of Ash vs Evil Dead, Bruce Campbell — who plays Ash in the television series and the two Evil Dead movies.
Campbell called on Conservative Nation to “check your facts” and said it was “sad” his co-star was made the face of a political hoax.
Neither McCurdy, who has also shared posts supporting Trump’s controversial plan to build a wall along the border with Mexico, nor Conservative Nation have removed the posts from their respective accounts despite the hoax being debunked.
The photo continues to be shared by people who are unaware it’s been proven to be fake.
Weaving wasn’t the only actress to fall victim to such a hoax. Pro-Trump social media accounts passed off Mexican telenovela actress Luisa Rubina as a 15-year-old victim of an attack by an anti-Trump mob.
The image of 17-year-old Rubina, with blood running from gashes on her face, are a still of a scene from the Univision soap opera La Rosa de Guadelupe.
Rubino told Fusion, which is owned by Univision, that she most certainly was not beaten up at the San Jose rally.
“The caption says I was beaten up by anti-Trump people, as if I were a Trump supporter. And that’s not the case because I’m Mexican and I support the Latino community.”
In the year since he declared his presidential aspirations, Trump has been accused of inciting hatred and in the past week high-ranking members of his own party said statements Trump made about a U.S. judge, of Mexican heritage, were “racist”.
image_captions: This is not a Donald Trump supporter who was beaten up at a rally; it's Australian actress Samara Weaving covered in fake blood on the set of the Starz series 'Ash vs. Evil Dead.Samara Weaving/Instagram
This is not a Donald Trump supporter who was beaten up at a rally; it's Australian actress Samara Weaving covered in fake blood on the set of the Starz series 'Ash vs. Evil Dead.
